channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Florida Supreme Court ruling on abortion amendment expected today
item.description: Florida Supreme Court justices are expected to issue a ruling Thursday on an abortion rights amendment that may appear on the 2024 ballot.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: US congressional delegation reaffirms defense support for Taiwan
item.description: A bipartisan U.S. congressional delegation has affirmed continued support for Taiwan following recent approval of $300 million in military aid, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Gridlock expected as Biden, Obama and Clinton arrive in NYC for fundraiser
item.description: MANHATTAN, N.Y. (PIX11) – President Joe Biden and former Presidents Barack Obama and Bill Clinton will be in New York City Thursday for a fundraiser. The event will be held at Radio City Music Hall. Road closures are expected during the fundraiser.
item.dc:creator: Julie Millet, PIX 11

item.title: Obama, Clinton to attend star-studded Biden fundraiser in NYC
item.description: The event will feature other big names, including late night comedy host Stephen Colbert, Queen Latifah and Lizzo.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: Chris Christie withdraws from consideration for ‘No Labels’ presidential run
item.description: Christ Christie turned down an offer to run a third party presidential campaign under the No Labels title on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Taking a cue from the pews, Texas churches expanding mental health services
item.description: Many Texas faith groups have offered counseling services in the past. But more of them are expanding their programs, partnering with providers, to help meet the growing need here for mental health services.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, Stephen Simpson, KPRC 2

item.title: A woman’s fight to escape the hospital shows Medicaid’s limits for disabled Texans
item.description: Staffing shortages and mismanaged care can delay when Texans on some Medicaid programs are discharged from hospitals. This can cost the state more and take a toll on patients and caregivers.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, Neelam Bohra, KPRC 2

item.title: White House unveils new AI regulations for federal agencies
item.description: The Biden administration's Office of Management and Budget (OMB) rolled out new artificial intelligence regulations for federal agencies.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bill Clinton, Barack Obama, raise a $25 million bulwark for Biden as Dems fret over Trump poll advantage
item.description: Former Presidents Bill Clinton and Barack Obama help Joe Biden haul in $25 million in fundraising for his 2024 election rematch with Donald Trump
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Republican committee to select Buck's likely replacement, adding a challenge to Boebert's campaign
item.description: DENVER (AP) — A panel of Colorado Republicans will select a candidate Thursday who will likely serve out the final months of U.S. Rep. Ken Buck ’s term — and could pose a challenge to Rep. Lauren Boebert’s bid for another term in Congress.
item.dc:creator: JESSE BEDAYN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge forges ahead with pretrial motions in Georgia election interference case
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — The charges against former President Donald Trump in the Georgia election interference case seek to criminalize political speech and advocacy conduct that the First Amendment protects, his lawyers argued in a court filing challenging the indictment.
item.dc:creator: KATE BRUMBACK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Cook County State's Attorney election still too close to call
item.description: CHICAGO — It has been a week and a day since the polls closed for the Illinois Primaries, but there's still no clear winner in the Democratic Primary for Cook County State's Attorney. The race between Eileen O'Neill Burke and Clayton Harris III remains razor-thin.
item.dc:creator: Andrea Medina, WGN 9

item.title: California judge recommends disbarment of pro-Trump attorney John Eastman
item.description: A California judge found that attorney John Eastman committed "exceptionally serious ethical violations" in his efforts to overturn the results of the 2020 election and recommended disbarment.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dreisbach, MPR News

item.title: Republicans’ budding interest in Texas’ housing crisis could create strange political bedfellows
item.description: Republican lawmakers may try to force Texas cities to allow greater housing density as the state faces a housing crunch.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, Joshua Fechter, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump mocks Ronna McDaniel after ouster from NBC
item.description: Former President Trump made fun of Ronna McDaniel after NBC News ended its contract with her following an internal uproar across the company.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, NewsNation

item.title: Cash-strapped Trump is now selling $60 Bibles, U.S. Constitution included
item.description: The “God Bless The USA” Bible is inspired by the Lee Greenwood anthem and includes copies of the nation’s founding documents. Trump launched it ahead of Easter as his financial woes mount.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman, MPR News

item.title: DeSantis signs bill to increase penalties for squatters, protect homeowners in Florida
item.description: Governor Ron DeSantis signed House Bill 621 in Orlando on Wednesday, ending the squatters scam in the state of Florida.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Minnesota electric vehicle rebates coming to qualified car buyers starting in April
item.description: Most of the money in a Minnesota electric vehicle rebate program has been claimed and checks will start going out in late April.
item.dc:creator: Mark Zdechlik, MPR News

item.title: Settlement reached in lawsuit between Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis allies and Disney
item.description: Allies of Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis and Disney reached a settlement agreement Wednesday in a lawsuit over who controls Walt Disney World’s governing district.
item.dc:creator: Mike Schneider, WFLA, NBC

item.title: MTA expected to vote on final congestion pricing rates at board meeting
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – It’s been a long time coming and has faced a lot of pushback, but we may have a final answer on the MTA’s controversial congestion pricing plan on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Kiran Dhillon, PIX 11

item.title: Democrats ramp up attacks on RFK Jr.: 'He should be ashamed of himself'
item.description: Democrats are stepping up their criticism of Robert F. Kennedy Jr. after he announced his pick of Nicole Shanahan for vice president Tuesday, arguing the duo will benefit former President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Hanna Trudo, NewsNation

item.title: Democrat wins Alabama special election after focusing on IVF, abortion
item.description: Democratic candidate Marilyn Lands has won the special election in Alabama state House District 10, according to a projection from Decision Desk HQ. Lands, a licensed professional counselor, beat Republican Teddy Powell, a Madison City Council member, to fill the seat.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, NewsNation

item.title: Trump seeks to capitalize on Biden’s frustration with Israel’s Netanyahu
item.description: Support for Israel's war in Gaza has fractured along political and religious lines. But Trump's own remarks about Jewish Americans have been sharply criticized.
item.dc:creator: Franco Ordoñez, MPR News

item.title: Trump's social media company surges in trading debut in a big boost to Trump's wealth
item.description: Trump Media and Technology Group surged in its trading debut, substantially boosting the former president's wealth — at least on paper.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Nam, MPR News

item.title: Bring Chicago Home backers retreat and regroup
item.description: CHICAGO — Days after Chicago voters said no to raising the real estate transfer tax on property sales over $1 million to combat homelessness in the city, supporters of the measure struck a tone of humility.
item.dc:creator: Tahman Bradley, WGN 9

item.title: Trump is selling 'God Bless USA' Bibles
item.description: The $60 Bible will include a copy of Greenwood’s “God Bless the U.S.A.,” as well as the U.S. Constitution, the Bill of Rights, the Declaration of Independence and the Pledge of Allegiance.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Legislation would break out $45 million for Hoover Dam locked up by red tape
item.description: When Hoover Dam was built in the 1930s, it cost $49 million. Now, congressional leaders are mounting an effort to "free up" $45 million in the Colorado River Dam fund that has been "inaccessible for decades due to bureaucracy and federal red tape.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: NY Republican says House could 'end up having a Speaker Hakeem Jeffries' as GOP majority narrows
item.description: Rep. Claudia Tenney (R-N.Y.) raised concerns Monday that a dwindling House GOP majority due to multiple vacancies could hurt the caucus’ prospects and may even lead to Democrats taking over the chamber.
item.dc:creator: Nick Robertson, Fox 5

item.title: Senate GOP cautions Trump on 15-week national abortion ban
item.description: Senate Republicans are warning former President Trump that restrictions on abortion should be left to the states and that proposing a 15-week national abortion ban ahead of this year’s election would be a major political blunder.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: Alabama special election to test political potency of abortion, IVF
item.description: A special election in an unusual purple Alabama state House district is testing the political potency of reproductive rights in a red state after a major ruling there last month led to a temporary pause on in vitro fertilization (IVF) treatment.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, Fox 5

item.title: Court agrees to pause collection of Trump's massive civil fraud judgment if he puts up $175M
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A New York appeals court on Monday agreed to hold off collection of former President Donald Trump’s more than $454 million civil fraud judgment if he puts up $175 million within 10 days.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump's New York hush money case is set for trial April 15
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The first of Donald Trump’s four criminal trials will begin April 15, a Manhattan judge ruled Monday, tearing into the former president’s lawyers for what he said were unfounded claims that the hush-money case had been tainted by prosecutorial misconduct. Judge Juan M.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, JAKE OFFENHARTZ and ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Can a candidate be replaced after winning the presidential primary?
item.description: Both presumptive candidates for the presidential election in 2024 will be at least 78 years old or older by Inauguration Day 2025. One is also charged in four criminal cases and facing 88 felony counts, which could lead the party to shift tactics pending a conviction.
item.dc:creator: Justin Kounelias, Fox 5

item.title: Mayor Adams meets alleged dealers at 'drug bazaar' Burger King: reports
item.description: MANHATTAN, N.Y. (PIX11) – Mayor Eric Adams met with alleged drug dealers at a Burger King described as a "drug bazaar" near City Hall, according to the New York Post. Adams stopped by the fast food location to assess the situation himself following a complaint, according to a City Hall spokesperson.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Democratic primary race for Cook County State’s Attorney remains too close to call; vote-by-mail ballots still being processed
item.description: CHICAGO — The gap is narrowing in the Democratic primary for Cook County State's Attorney, but the race was still too close to call as of Monday morning, as Chicago Board of Elections officials continue to process vote-by-mail ballots.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Nevada Secretary of State fires back, asks for more funding from Congress
item.description: Nevada Secretary of State Francisco Aguilar responded on Friday to a Republican congressman's request for an explanation why voters' history records showed they had voted in the presidential preference primary even though they had not cast a ballot.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Chicago voters reject ‘mansion tax’ to fund homeless services during Illinois primary
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Chicago voters have rejected a one-time real estate tax on properties over $1 million to pay for services for homeless people. It’s a loss for first-term Chicago Mayor Brandon Johnson, who championed the so-called “mansion tax.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9
